items.headline: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged, revises GDP and inflation forecasts for FY27
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) unanimously kept the policy repo rate unchanged at 5.25% in its June 2026 MPC review, with a 'Neutral' stance. The RBI lowered its real GDP growth forecast for FY27 to 6.6% from 6.9% and raised the CPI inflation forecast to 5.1% from 4.6% due to deteriorating global environment and rising domestic uncertainties.

items.headline: RBI and government introduce measures to attract foreign capital; India-UK FTA due July 15
items.summary: The RBI and government announced joint fiscal and regulatory measures to attract foreign capital, including expanding the Fully Accessible Route (FAR) for government securities, removing FPI investment limits under the General Route, and increasing equity caps for NRIs/OCIs. The India-UK free trade agreement (FTA) is set to take effect on July 15, expected to boost bilateral trade.

items.headline: US markets fall on Fed's hawkish stance; Asian markets show mixed trends
items.summary: US stock markets closed lower (S&P 500 down 1.21%, Nasdaq down 1.34%) after the Federal Reserve kept rates unchanged but signaled potential future hikes to combat inflation. Asian markets were mixed, with Japan's Nikkei and South Korea's KOSPI up over 1%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng and China's Shanghai Composite declined.

items.headline: Crude oil prices decline, FIIs net sellers while DIIs provide support
items.summary: International oil benchmarks declined, with Brent crude falling to $78.24 per barrel and US WTI crude to $75 per barrel, seen as positive for India. The Indian Rupee showed mild weakness. Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) were net sellers of ₹101.59 crore on June 17, 2026, while Domestic institutional investors (DIIs) were net buyers, injecting ₹1,561.40 crore.

items.headline: GB Logistics Commerce, Chandni Machines, Candour Techtex, Anik Industries to announce results today
items.summary: Several companies, including GB Logistics Commerce Ltd, Chandni Machines, Candour Techtex, and Anik Industries, are scheduled to announce their audited or quarterly results on June 18, 2026.

items.headline: Entertainment Network (India) Ltd (ENIL) reports Q4 FY26 revenue growth of 3.9%, digital business up 84%
items.summary: ENIL reported a 3.9% year-on-year revenue growth in Q4 FY26, with its digital business revenue impressively increasing by 84%. However, the radio segment faced challenges. The board recommended a dividend for FY26.

items.headline: Rail Vikas Nigam (RVNL) Q4 FY26 revenue up, but EBITDA drops 38%
items.summary: RVNL announced a 4.18% year-on-year increase in revenue from operations to ₹6,695.91 crore for Q4 FY26. However, its EBITDA dropped by 38.42% year-on-year to ₹269 crore, and the EBITDA margin contracted to 4.01%.

items.headline: IT stocks face pressure, while Consumer Durables, FMCG, Metal, and Chemicals gain in early trade
items.summary: In early trade today, the Nifty IT index declined by 1.70%. Conversely, Nifty Consumer Durables and Nifty FMCG both rose by 0.31% each, Nifty Metal advanced by 0.25%, and Nifty Chemicals gained 0.21%. PSU banking stocks remained in focus.

items.headline: Realty stocks lead market rally, Aquaculture sector sees biggest weekly decline
items.summary: Over the past week (ending June 15, 2026), realty stocks led the market rally with a gain of 3.55%. In contrast, the Aquaculture sector stocks were the biggest losers, dropping 3%.

items.headline: Several companies including Tata Technologies, JSW Infrastructure trade ex-dividend today
items.summary: On June 18, 2026, Capital Small Finance Bank, eMudhra, GHCL, HDB Financial Services, JSW Infrastructure, Monika Alcobev, Swastika Investmart, Tata Technologies (special and final dividends), and Vimta Labs are trading ex-dividend.

items.headline: HDFC Bank, Polycab India, IndiaMART InterMESH among those trading ex-dividend tomorrow
items.summary: A larger group of companies will trade ex-dividend on June 19, 2026, including Amba Enterprises, AWL Agri Business, Corona Remedies, GHCL Textiles, HDFC Bank, HDFC Life Insurance Company, IndiaMART InterMESH (special and final dividends), Polycab India, Tata Communications, Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles, and Torrent Power.

items.headline: String Metaverse bonus issue, Deepak Builders stock split scheduled for June 19
items.summary: String Metaverse will have its ex-date for a bonus issue in the ratio of 2:9 on June 19. Deepak Builders will also have its ex-date on June 19 for a stock split, changing shares from ₹10 to Re 1. Sangam (India) Limited is nearing its ex-dividend date around June 22nd for a dividend of ₹2.00 per share.

items.headline: Hexaware Technologies to expand UK operations; HFCL secures large order from RVNL
items.summary: Hexaware Technologies announced a £25 million investment to expand its UK operations, creating 1,200 jobs. HFCL secured a significant order worth ₹2,666 crore from Rail Vikas Nigam Ltd for the BharatNet Phase-3 project. RBI's temporary lifting of interest-rate caps on NRI deposits puts banking stocks like HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank in focus. BSE shares will be watched following NSE's preliminary IPO papers filing.

items.headline: Infosys, HCLTech, TCS, Tech Mahindra among Nifty's top losers today
items.summary: Today's top losers from the Nifty pack include major IT companies like Infosys, HCLTech, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Tech Mahindra, and Wipro, alongside Bajaj Finance and Bajaj Finserv.

items.headline: RVNL, HFCL, Gujrat Gas, Hudco, Lupin among stocks to watch today
items.summary: Stocks to watch today, June 18, include RVNL, HFCL, Gujrat Gas, Hudco, Lupin, Hexaware Technologies, ZF Commercial Vehicle Control Systems India, Lemon Tree Hotels, Anik Industries, and Balkrishna Industries.
